div.head: 220. Telegram 1624 to LondonSource: Department of State, Central Files, 611.93/9–2055. Confidential. Drafted by McConaughy; cleared by Sebald and Robertson. Repeated to Geneva for Johnson as telegram 772.
div.p: 1624. Your 1119. Department considers it mere Chinese Communist quibble whether GOI and UK should be “invited”, “notified” or “requested” to perform allotted functions. Department naturally communicated with both GOI and UKG in regard U.S. interest in their functions. It assumed PRC would do likewise.
PRC clearly obligated make arrangements necessary to enable UK Government function. U.S. Government promptly discharged its obligation to enable GOI to function.
